judge_constraints: REGRESSION DIAGNOSIS (completion bar: match must land WITHOUT this construct family): Construct `(s16)(a0 << 16 >> 16)` (line ~909, argument to func_8008BD88) fails Tests 1, 2, 3 of the 6-test checklist: a0 is already s16, the shift pair is mathematically a no-op for all valid s16 inputs (no semantic purpose), a human programmer from spec would write `a0` directly, and the sole justification is GCC-internals (forces sll+sra before jal). It is not in any explicitly forbidden catalog family (pure C, no asm/pins/volatile), but it is also NOT in the SOTN-accepted exceptions list. The 'opaque arithmetic' carve-out does not extend to this pattern without its own SOTN-master-branch evidence; each exception requires independent clearance per the standing policy. The function needs a clean redo: either confirm the sll+sra is genuinely in the target asm and that a direct `a0` argument also produces score=0 (in which case the shift is unnecessary and should be deleted), or if the shift IS required, surface a policy question to the owner before re-merging.  (committed code flagged by the re-audit patrol; review and re-do in pure C if confirmed. The byte-correct construct stays on main until a clean replacement lands.)
